headlines.description: — LaMelo Ball hit seven first-half 3-pointers in his return from a right ankle injury, Charlotte had 18 in the opening 24 minutes to match the NBA and franchise records and the Hornets beat the Atlanta Hawks 133-126 on Thursday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: LaMelo Ball hits 7 1st-half 3-pointers in return, Hornets beat Hawks 133-126

headlines.description: — Norman Powell scored 24 points, Kel’el Ware had 22 points, 12 rebounds and four blocks, and the Miami Heat snapped a five-game losing streak with a 106-95 victory over the Brooklyn Nets on Thursday night.

events.name: LA Clippers at Oklahoma City Thunder

headlines.description: — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 32 points before resting in the fourth quarter, and the Oklahoma City Thunder bounced back from a rare loss to beat the Los Angeles Clippers 122-101 on Thursday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 32 as Thunder bounce back from rare loss with 122-101 win over Clippers

events.name: Houston Rockets at New Orleans Pelicans

headlines.description: — Saddiq Bey scored 21 of his season-high 29 points after halftime, and the New Orleans Pelicans overcame a 25-point, third-quarter deficit to beat the Houston Rockets 133-128 in overtime on Thursday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bey has season-high 29 points to lead Pelicans' 25-point comeback in a 133-128 OT win over Houston

headlines.description: — Nikola Jokic had 23 points, 13 assists and 11 rebounds to become the career assists leader among centers, and the streaking Denver Nuggets beat the Orlando Magic 126-115 on Thursday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jokic sets the career record for assists by a center as the Nuggets beat the Magic 126-115

events.name: Sacramento Kings at Portland Trail Blazers

headlines.description: — Deni Avdija made two free throws with 1.5 seconds left in overtime for the last of his 35 points, and the Portland Trail Blazers outlasted the Sacramento Kings 134-133 on Thursday night to open a home-and-home set.
headlines.shortLinkText: Trail Blazers outlast Kings 134-133 in overtime on Avdija's late free throws
